concepts.title: Definition of Oscillation
concepts.description: Oscillation is the repeated back and forth movement of an object around its equilibrium position. Understanding this concept is crucial for the entire chapter.

concepts.title: Simple Harmonic Motion (SHM)
concepts.description: SHM is a special type of oscillation where the force acting on the object is directly proportional to its displacement from the equilibrium position, and the motion is sinusoidal in nature.

concepts.title: Equation of SHM
concepts.description: The equation of SHM is given by x(t) = A sin(ωt + φ), where A is the amplitude, ω is the angular frequency, and φ is the phase angle. This equation is frequently asked in exams.

concepts.title: Remembering SHM Equations
concepts.description: Use the mnemonic 'AWESOME' to remember the SHM equations: A (amplitude), W (angular frequency), E (equilibrium position), S (simple harmonic motion), O (oscillation), M (mass), E (energy).

concepts.title: Confusing Frequency and Angular Frequency
concepts.description: Students often confuse frequency (f) with angular frequency (ω). To avoid this, remember that ω = 2πf, and make sure to use the correct unit (rad/s for ω and Hz for f).

concepts.title: Units of Oscillation
concepts.description: Remember the units of important quantities in oscillation: amplitude (m), frequency (Hz), angular frequency (rad/s), time period (s), and phase angle (rad).

concepts.title: Types of Oscillation
concepts.description: There are two main types of oscillation: free oscillation (where the system oscillates without any external force) and forced oscillation (where an external force is applied to the system).

concepts.title: Resonance
concepts.description: Resonance occurs when the frequency of the external force matches the natural frequency of the system, resulting in maximum amplitude. This concept is frequently tested in exams.
